# awscc_devopsagent_service (Resource)

The AWS::DevOpsAgent::Service resource registers external services (like Dynatrace, MCP servers, GitLab) for integration with DevOpsAgent.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `service_type` (String) The type of service being registered

### Optional

- `kms_key_arn` (String) The ARN of the KMS key to use for encryption.
- `service_details` (Attributes) Service-specific configuration details for create operation (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details))
- `tags` (Attributes Set) An array of key-value pairs to apply to this resource. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--tags))

### Read-Only

- `accessible_resources` (List of Map of String) List of accessible resources for this service
- `additional_service_details` (Attributes) Additional details specific to the service type (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--additional_service_details))
- `arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Service.
- `id` (String) Uniquely identifies the resource.
- `service_id` (String) The unique identifier of the service

<a id="nestedatt--service_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_details`

Optional:

- `azure_identity` (Attributes) Azure Identity service configuration for federated identity (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--azure_identity))
- `dynatrace` (Attributes) Dynatrace service configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--dynatrace))
- `git_lab` (Attributes) GitLab service configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--git_lab))
- `mcp_server` (Attributes) MCP server configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server))
- `mcp_server_grafana` (Attributes) Grafana MCP server configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_grafana))
- `mcp_server_new_relic` (Attributes) New Relic service configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_new_relic))
- `mcp_server_sig_v4` (Attributes) SigV4-authenticated MCP server configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_sig_v4))
- `mcp_server_splunk` (Attributes) Splunk MCP server configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_splunk))
- `pager_duty` (Attributes) PagerDuty service configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--pager_duty))
- `service_now` (Attributes) ServiceNow service configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--service_now))

<a id="nestedatt--service_details--azure_identity"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_details.azure_identity`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) Azure AD application client ID
- `tenant_id` (String) Azure AD tenant ID
- `web_identity_role_arn` (String) ARN of the IAM role for web identity token exchange
- `web_identity_token_audiences` (List of String) List of audiences for the web identity token

### Nested Schema for `service_details.dynatrace`

Optional:

- `account_urn` (String) Dynatrace resource account URN
- `authorization_config` (Attributes) Dynatrace OAuth authorization configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--dynatrace--authorization_config))

### Nested Schema for `service_details.dynatrace.authorization_config`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) OAuth client ID
- `client_name` (String) User friendly OAuth client name
- `client_secret` (String) OAuth client secret
- `exchange_parameters` (String) OAuth token exchange parameters




<a id="nestedatt--service_details--git_lab"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_details.git_lab`

Optional:

- `group_id` (String) Optional GitLab group ID for group-level access tokens
- `target_url` (String) GitLab instance URL
- `token_type` (String) Type of GitLab access token
- `token_value` (String) GitLab access token value


<a id="nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server"></a>

Optional:

- `description` (String) Optional description for the MCP server
- `endpoint` (String) MCP server endpoint URL
- `name` (String) MCP server name

### Nested Schema for `service_details.mcp_server.authorization_config`

Optional:

- `api_key` (Attributes) API key authentication details (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server--authorization_config--api_key))
- `o_auth_client_credentials` (Attributes) MCP server OAuth client credentials configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server--authorization_config--o_auth_client_credentials))

- `api_key_value` (String) API key value

- `client_secret` (String) OAuth client secret
- `exchange_parameters` (String) OAuth token exchange parameters
- `exchange_url` (String) OAuth token exchange URL
- `scopes` (List of String) OAuth scopes

### Nested Schema for `service_details.mcp_server_new_relic.authorization_config.api_key`

Optional:

- `account_id` (String) New Relic Account ID
- `alert_policy_ids` (List of String) List of alert policy IDs
- `api_key` (String) New Relic User API Key
- `application_ids` (List of String) List of monitored APM application IDs
- `entity_guids` (List of String) List of globally unique IDs for New Relic resources
- `region` (String) New Relic region




<a id="nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_sig_v4"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_details.mcp_server_sig_v4`

Optional:

- `authorization_config` (Attributes) SigV4 authorization configuration for MCP server (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_sig_v4--authorization_config))
- `description` (String) Optional description for the MCP server
- `endpoint` (String) MCP server endpoint URL
- `name` (String) MCP server name

<a id="nestedatt--service_details--mcp_server_sig_v4--authorization_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_details.mcp_server_sig_v4.authorization_config`

Optional:

- `custom_headers` (Map of String) Custom headers for the SigV4 MCP server
- `mcp_role_arn` (String) IAM role ARN to assume for SigV4 signing. Optional - when omitted, credentials are resolved at runtime via a monitor account association.
- `region` (String) AWS region for SigV4 signing. Use '*' for SigV4a multi-region signing.
- `role_arn` (String) Deprecated - use McpRoleArn instead. IAM role ARN to assume for SigV4 signing
- `service` (String) AWS service name for SigV4 signing

- `name` (String) MCP server name

- `mcp_server_splunk` (Attributes) MCP server details returned after registration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--additional_service_details--mcp_server_splunk))

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) Optional description for the MCP server

- `name` (String) MCP server name


<a id="nestedatt--additional_service_details--mcp_server_grafana"></a>

### Nested Schema for `additional_service_details.mcp_server_new_relic`

Read-Only:

- `account_id` (String) New Relic account ID

- `region` (String) New Relic region


<a id="nestedatt--additional_service_details--mcp_server_sig_v4"></a>
### Nested Schema for `additional_service_details.mcp_server_sig_v4`

Read-Only:

- `custom_headers` (Map of String) Custom headers for the SigV4 MCP server
- `description` (String) Optional description for the MCP server
- `endpoint` (String) The MCP server endpoint URL
- `mcp_role_arn` (String) IAM role ARN for SigV4 signing. Absent when no dedicated role is configured.
- `name` (String) The MCP server name
- `region` (String) AWS region for SigV4 signing
- `role_arn` (String) Deprecated - use McpRoleArn instead. IAM role ARN for SigV4 signing
- `service` (String) AWS service name for SigV4 signing

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.12.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `identity` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_devopsagent_service.example
  identity = {
    service_id = "service_id"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
### Identity Schema

#### Required

- `service_id` (String) The unique identifier of the service

#### Optional

- `account_id` (String) AWS Account where this resource is managed
- `region` (String) Region where this resource is managed

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = awscc_devopsagent_service.example
  id = "service_id"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
$ terraform import awscc_devopsagent_service.example "service_id"
```
